title: Link-Column with a Local Link (not Web-Link)

posts.author: m.gruber (Michael Gruber)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

Our Issue:
We have many tasks that are associated with a large number of files. Those files are all saved on a local network-share. I want to be able to easily access that folder in Monday. It is not practical to attach every single file to a task (too many files, also they have to be accessible by other applications). I’d prefer to have a simple link to our local path, to be opened with Windows Explorer. The available “Link-column” does not work, as every link generated will start with a “http:” will therefore be opened in the browser.

It would be nice to have a column very similar to the link-column, but with a local path, e.g. “X:\project\customer1\”, that then opens in Windows Explorer.

Our Workaround:
We currently use a normal Text-column with the link as plain Text, so we can copy/paste it to Windows Explorer. This works, but feels a little clumsy, especially when the link is very long.

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love the same feature. We’re currently using the exact same copy/paste workaround, and it’s definitely more time consuming.

posts.author: IanFury (Ian Fury)
posts.cookedHtml: Along similar lines but somewhat more niche. I would really appreciate being able to link to Bentley ProjectWise. I work in the Rail Industry and there are all kinds of sensitivities about Intellectual Property and security around various types of information (e.g. Drawings). Monday is great for creating whatever workflow I want and managing issues but as a project we could get into trouble for allowing people to share certain information. ProjectWise is the defacto drawing management system for all Infrastructure Projects. ProjectWise links work fine in MS Word and Excel but that is because they are talking to the ProjectWise client application on the local PC. There is a work around that is a bit clunky. Save links into a .htm file (web page) and add to the files section of a pulse. Depending on your browser settings, clicking on the file will open up a new tab in which the link will work (becase the file has been downloaded to your PC as a temporary file). This works for local PC or local Network links.

posts.author: Marko (Marko Petrović)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have exact same problem.

I recently submitted this idea as feature request, unfortunately i wasnt aware of community forum until few days ago.

This feature would make our life so much easier. We have a lot of project documentation and 3D models which are to large (and also need constant editing until project is finished) to be able to upload them to Monday.com. This would make our life much easier and make full transition to Monday.com (since we are still in “building” phase with idea to expand it to rest of the company step by step) much easier and faster!

I though about same workaround, but due to number of files per project and quite log links from internal storage and NAS its not practical at all.

posts.author: IanFury (Ian Fury)
posts.cookedHtml: It seems to me that if you are using NAS Monday.com should make it possible to build links from component parts using data in columns. I know it can’t do this but it would be good if it could. So one column would contain the name of the route level directory, another the subfolder, another the filename. A special new local link column would build the link for each pulse based on the component info. You could use Automations based on Group Names to to populate directory names…

posts.author: cspowers (Carolyn Powers)
posts.cookedHtml: We would also love this added as a feature for our use case-- 90% of our project handoffs involve linking to project folders in our windows environment. Even just the option to not auto input the http:// and instead allow for file:// *should work in chrome. This could be a pretty easy feature to add.

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also want to remove the automatic https for another work around.

Currently the mailto links don’t work because they have a http forced infront of them.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Link column should definitely be able to link to local links. We are using a NAS Synology and de links in the column are not clickable which is a waste of time (having to copy paste the inserted url). Slack does that really wel and it doesn’t look like a hard thing to implement.

On top of the clickable local links I would love to see multple links in a link column Multiple links in link column

posts.author: John_McConnachie (John)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this moved on, as its Feb 2021 and I cant find a way to do this still. I too am working with files that are too big to upload and I am not sure that my company would be happy with storing files offsite but would love to have a way to have a clickable link that opens up the files directly.

posts.author: tpollet (Thomas POLLET)
posts.cookedHtml:
We could use that too as we currently do the exact same thing, which is copy-paste the link in a texte column…
It works but it feels more like hacking than right use and is not comfortable…

posts.author: optoprojects (OPTO Projects)
posts.cookedHtml: We would also like this feature please.

posts.author: MHaigh (Matthew Haigh)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday Dev Team… there is an EASY win here… please update the verification on the LINK column to allow file:// rdp:// ftp:// smb:// sftp:// tel:// skype:// etc etc … these would then all be supported directly by the browser, so EASY change for you to make and would make the link column much more flexible…

posts.author: DavidSkotte (David)
posts.cookedHtml: Our team is also looking for this feature! Please add this Monday…

posts.author: CGI (Sany)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any news on this feature?
We could really do with it too.

posts.author: MHaigh (Matthew Haigh)
posts.cookedHtml: I believe Monday have updated the LINK column, so some additional links will now work… I haven’t tested or seen a formal list from Monday as to what links will now work, but you should find it has more capability ! [Thank you Monday Dev Team! ]

posts.author: mpm0rgan (Matthew Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: Still does not address what folks here are asking for (at least I certainly couldn’t get it working with file://) Please if I am wrong, I would love to know what I am doing wrong. I would very much like to be able to use such functionality!

posts.author: SamH (Sam Haeverans)
posts.cookedHtml:
I tried to use it for opening links to our ERP system: (Ridder-links : starting with Ridder://).
This works in Browser (Chrome) but not in the desktop app.
An update for that would be welcome!

posts.author: rickwhaas (Rick)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates to this? We need the same functionality where we create a link which opens a folder within folder explorer.

posts.author: josbri (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml: Wondering what the status of this is. I see the request multiple times by users but no response from Monday.com that I can see. Is this a feature Monday is actually considering or no?

posts.author: matmid01 (Matthias)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here - would be great to have that feature.

posts.author: mviquez (Michael Viquez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi There! I do have a work around for everyone.
First: Right click on the folder or file you want to link, and select send a link to this folder

This is meant to send via Ourlook, but it also allows you to copy the link.

Then you can paste that into a link column.

image571×572 20.3 KB

I hope that works for everyone.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: This of course only works when you use Zeedrive.

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml: HI @monday-team @CSG @monday-team Any update if this is going to be actioned as would be useful as most of our files are stored on a network drive?

posts.author: mike.newman (Mike Newman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our company has been waiting years for this function. Same as others: big files on local network, that are frequently updated, in formats that don’t preview. Such as AutoCAD DWG and RVT.

We’ve been on a slow rollout for over a year and finally getting traction but this is a sticking point for our users. We ask them to cut and paste the path from the location bar and then append the file name. Both by copy-paste, it renders a dumb text link that others can copy-paste in their own explorer window. Clunky and error-prone.

Our staff are trained to use file-linking in Outlook using CTRL+K on the keyboard. Telling them Monday.com doesn’t have parallel functionality perpetuates their behavior of sending file links via email instead of updating the conversation in Monday.

posts.author: Becker_L (Lars)
posts.cookedHtml:
It should be possible to integrated this funkction easily. The added value would be huge.
This missing is one of the reasons our company has not yet switched to monday.

posts.author: CraigA (Craig Anstey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has a work around been created for this? As others in this thread we have project files that are too large to upload direct to MDC. We are using MDC to streamline our business but this is currently causing us a big issue.

Does anyone from Monday.com ever respond to these threads as I cannot see any??

posts.author: MichWing (Michelle Winger)
posts.cookedHtml: Also looking for this feature in our Company. Monday development team, please help out!

posts.author: StephenSell (Stephen Sell)
posts.cookedHtml: Much needed feature

posts.author: FMdenHeijer (Malan den Heijer)
posts.cookedHtml: Really need this feature as well. All our work is on Drive network folders

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my voice to this thread. Also have a lot of local network files linked to projects. Would love to be able to link to the network path for quick access. @BiancaT Would love if you could get the attention of the development team to consider this one in the next round of items to be added to the Roadmap

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Terry.Heard

Don’t think that is ever going to happen. When it would be possible to link to local files (residing on your PC, server) how would other users have access to those files? Those other users might not have access to your PC, server or whatever device you are storing files locally. The solution is to upload those local files to somewhere where other users can access them (like Gdrive or OneDrive).

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I have to disagree. Why wouldn’t it be possible to link to a folder on a shared server? We do that all the time. We do have to share the path manually, but we have absolutely no problem sharing it.

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml: We all work in the same office, not remote sites. So all users do have access to the files from their computers. It would just be a time efficiency improvement in being able to link directly to the files for each project.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: That might be the case in your situation, but I don’t think monday will support this as many other accounts will have users working from different offices. I am only trying to put some rational behind this, the final word has to come from monday.

posts.author: Becker_L (Lars)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are working from all over the world, but we are working with the same server and the folder is the same on each Laptop. As we work with very sensible Data we need to use the local file structure with encryption.
You can perfectly link the files in Excel, so that it works on every Laptop. We just need this feature in Monday as well.

posts.author: MichWing (Michelle Winger)
posts.cookedHtml:
I see where you’re coming from, but Outlook offers this feature which our company takes advantage of all the time. We put hyperlinks to a specific file from our server into an email all the time in order to share files with one another. The great thing about using a link in an email too instead of just uploading the file is that the link offers a dynamic access to the file where any changes you make to the file even after sending a link will be captured and the link will always remain the same (so long as the file path doesn’t change) whereas a file attached to the email is stagnant once it’s been attached meaning you would have to make changes and save and send it back and forth if you had multiple people working on it.
If Outlook can offer this feature of providing a link to local files, all well knowing that there will be times when someone outside of the organization would not be able to access the file being linked, why couldn’t Monday.com do the same?

posts.author: isdenn (Dennis Schlösser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,
this seems a good use for https://www.linkyourfile.com/

It is a mac and windows extension that allows you to create an https link for a local file or folder. If you click the link it will automatically open the file or folder you linked if it is available locally.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: This actually seems like a fair option to ease workflows.

posts.author: Boom929 (Brian Richardson)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my request to this as well - this feature would be extremely helpful for our workflow.

posts.author: artkurat (Benjamin Sen)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also looking for a function to link a folder or a file within monday to get directly into the macOs-Finder without clicking through many folder.

posts.author: artkurat (Benjamin Sen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any chance to create a link in our monday tool which i can redirect to a folder.

Background: We are using monday as a creative agency. In projects we are working on customer files and need to open the folders always new. If there would be a chance to just once put in the Folder-Link into monday (in an own column) it would save us some time. If i click on the link, the finder needs to open the folder i set in the link

posts.author: isdenn (Dennis Schlösser)
posts.cookedHtml: Maybe checkout the website / tool I posted above. It does exactly this. You can create a weblink to a folder in the finder. If you click the link on monday it will automatically open the finder in the defined folder if it is still available locally.

posts.author: MichWing (Michelle Winger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for posting as this may be a helpful option for some. However, utilizing this tool in our company would have a number of drawbacks including vetting yet another tool for Compliance and Privacy minimum standards. It also would mean an added cost to the company as it appears as though there is a 7-day free trial but after that the program must be purchased to use. The reality is that this should be an option that is native to Monday.com just as it is native to Outlook.com.

image1031×471 39.2 KB

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree that monday should expand its basic features. To be fair to Dennis, this is a one time purchase and somehow development cost need to be reimbursed.

posts.author: Po77sy (Peter Potts)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature would make of office based workflow so simple come on Monday

We have this in our excel board which navigates network folder-customer folder-job folder
We’re desperate for this simple feature

posts.author: isdenn (Dennis Schlösser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, it sounds like “Link Your File” is the right tool for you. It does exactly what you need. You can create clickable https links from network drives and paste them here on Monday. The links work on mac and windows devices. No need for an extra excel sheet for link management.

We launched on Product Hunt today and give a 30% discount with coupon code PHT30. Maybe give it a try.

posts.author: GGLEBRE (Guilherme Lebre)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m also looking for the functionality. Having a link to either open a file or a folder within the desktop.
Because we have a cloud based folder and they all stored with the same path on different computer across all team members, the links are static and the same for all team members.

posts.author: sbarlow (Steve Barlow)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this functionality.

posts.author: Mark_Carlson (Mark Carlson)
posts.cookedHtml: I am a wheelchair user and like to try and keep things simple all this is HurtingMyHead, think my meds are wrong, having a go with LongPathTool to fix the fix size thing, is it really that simple ? ‘columns’ I ask you !

posts.author: lashi (Lashi Bandara)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! We’ve built a monday.com app called Local and web links, specifically for this. You can open local files and folders directly without leaving monday.com.
It’s currently in beta, and you’re welcome to try it.

It will be available in the marketplace soon. We designed it to be very affordable , and its simple nature allows us to offer it at a low price.

viskode.com

Local and Web Links

Local & Web Links

You can really feel the gap between Monday and the local file system closing with this app. It’s especially useful for teams working with a shared network drive.
